examples.name: time_trend_pembrolizumab
examples.title: Pembrolizumab trials per year since 2018
examples.note: time_trend → time_series: annual count of pembrolizumab trials since 2018 — how research activity for the drug has grown year over year.

request.query: How has the number of trials for pembrolizumab changed per year since 2018?

meta.query_interpretation: Annual count of pembrolizumab trials since 2018.
assumptions: Interpreted 'since 2018' as start_year >= 2018, bucketed by year.
Bounded end_year at the current year so not-yet-started trials are excluded.
warnings: 5 study(ies) had unparseable or missing start dates and were excluded from the time trend

examples.name: phase_distribution_melanoma
examples.title: Melanoma trials by clinical trial phase
examples.note: categorical_distribution → bar_chart: how melanoma trials split across Phase 1–4 — where the pipeline is concentrated.

request.query: What is the distribution of melanoma trials across clinical trial phases?

visualization.title: Melanoma trials by clinical trial phase

vega_spec.title: Melanoma trials by clinical trial phase

hints.note: Phases shown in canonical order, not by height.

meta.query_interpretation: Distribution of melanoma trials across clinical trial phases.

examples.name: sponsor_comparison_conditions
examples.title: Lead sponsor class across melanoma vs lung cancer
examples.note: comparison → grouped_bar_chart: sponsor-class mix (industry, NIH, other) for melanoma vs lung cancer — who funds research in each.

request.query: Compare the types of sponsors running melanoma trials versus lung cancer trials.

visualization.title: Lead sponsor class across melanoma vs lung cancer

vega_spec.title: Lead sponsor class across melanoma vs lung cancer

hints.note: Grouped bars: one cluster per category, one bar per series value.

meta.query_interpretation: Comparison of sponsor types (lead sponsor class) running melanoma versus lung cancer trials.

examples.name: sponsor_drug_network_melanoma
examples.title: Network of sponsors and drugs co-occurring in melanoma trials.
examples.note: cooccurrence_network → network_graph: sponsors linked to the drugs they study in melanoma trials; every node and edge carries its source NCT ids.

request.query: Show the network of sponsors and drugs that co-occur in melanoma trials.

visualization.title: Network of sponsors and drugs co-occurring in melanoma trials.

examples.name: geo_recruiting_melanoma
examples.title: Melanoma trials by country
examples.note: geographic_distribution → choropleth_map: recruiting-melanoma-trial counts by country (falls back to a ranked bar chart if a name can't be mapped).

request.query: Which countries have the most actively recruiting melanoma trials?

hints.note: Singapore, Hong Kong are not shown on the map (no country boundary in the world-110m basemap); the counts remain in the underlying data.

meta.query_interpretation: Countries with the most actively recruiting melanoma trials.

examples.name: enrollment_distribution_melanoma
examples.title: Distribution of enrollment count for melanoma
examples.note: numeric_distribution → histogram: how melanoma trials distribute across enrollment-size bins — most are small, a long tail is large.

request.query: What is the distribution of enrollment sizes for melanoma trials?

visualization.title: Distribution of enrollment count for melanoma

vega_spec.title: Distribution of enrollment count for melanoma

hints.note: Bins pre-computed in the transform layer.

meta.query_interpretation: Distribution of enrollment sizes for melanoma trials.
